content: Onmund: You’re not one of the usual apprentices. Those… uh… garments suggest you’re not here for magical studies. Lydia: Ha! Magic’s for milk-drinkers who can’t swing a blade. I’m here with my Thane, protecting him from the wretched bandit cunts who want to milk dry. You ever been ambushed by a gang of lust-drunk marauders who just want to chain you up and ride you till dawn? Onmund: ... By the Eight, no. I prefer my enemies , not… Lydia: Weak. A real Nord makes their enemies scream under them before silencing them forever. That’s what Dibella wills. You College types wouldn’t understand—too busy hiding behind wards while real warriors spill blood seed beneath the stars. Onmund: And yet didn’t freeze my bare tits off in Dawnstar last winter because I know a damn Flame Cloak spell. How’s frostbite on the nipples treating your battle fervor? Lydia: Spoken like a coward who’s never felt the glory of clashing steel under the northern lights with the wind biting every inch of your skin. But sure, keep burning your fingers like a tavern cook. Just don’t come crying when those Thalmor pricks invade—your little sparks won’t stop them from every Nord tradition. Onmund: Maybe. But I turn invisible and slip a lightning bolt up a Justiciar’s ass before he even chants “Talos. ” Ever tried that with your greatsword? Lydia: I don’t need tricks. I split them in half and let choke on my steel. Hmph. Bet your family’s proud—sneaking around like a Falmer instead of standing your ground. Onmund: Don’t. Talk about my family. Lydia: Oh-ho! So the reason you’re here? Daddy didn’t love his little spell-scribbler? Pathetic.

Onmund: You know what? Keep swinging that sword, Lydia. Maybe one day you’ll hit something besides your own pride. Lydia: And maybe one day you’ll cast something stronger than a fucking candlelight. Until then, enjoy freezing your balls off in your little robe while real warriors bleed for Skyrim.

Onmund: Gods, this ruin gives me the creeps. Feels like the dead could wake up and fuck us just as easily as they'd rip our throats out. Serana: Oh, relax. Most draugr just want to get laid before they turn to dust. That’s why they moan so much. Speaking of which—you ever fucked one? Onmund: What?! No—gross! I may be open-minded about magic, but I draw the line at cold, rotting corpses. Serana: Pfft. Live a little. You Nords are so uptight. MJR once fucked a Falmer so hard it forgot it was blind. Even though they don’t believe in gods—lucky for them, since Dibella would’ve struck them down for bad pussy. Onmund: Look, I respect the College’s neutrality, but you’re making a real case for why normal folk hate magic-users. Serana: Please, you people hate us ‘cause we don’t swing warhammers and choke on Talos-worship. Meanwhile, you all fuck your cousins and call it a blessing. Onmund: And you vampires drink blood and act smug about it.

Brelyna Maryon: Ugh, can you believe the stench in this ruin? Smells like Draugr piss and old, sweaty corpses. Makes me want to light something on fire just to cover it up. Onmund: Yeah, and the way those Draugr moan when they wake up... fucking unsettling. Sounds like they haven’t gotten laid in centuries. Brelyna Maryon: Knowing some of the bandits out here, they probably —dragged some poor milk-drunker down here for a nasty tomb romp. Onmund: Don’t remind me. repurpose Brelyna Maryon: Maybe that’s what Ancano gets up to when he sneaks off—banging ancient skeletons while whispering Thalmor secrets into their hollow ribs. Onmund: Bet he’s pissed none of them can talk back. Or maybe that’s his type—cold, dead, and totally obedient. Brelyna Maryon: Speaking of arrogant pricks, you ever notice how Master Tolfdir finds a way to stand just a little too close behind female apprentices? Onmund: Oh, by Talos, yes. Old bastard still thinks his Restoration spells can fix more than just stamina. Brelyna Maryon: Wish I could turn him into a sweetroll for an hour. See how many hands grope then. Onmund: Dibella’s tits, you’re vicious. Maybe that’s why your family shipped you here—too much fire in those gray fingers. Brelyna Maryon: And yet the one specializing in shock spells. Bet you zap a partner just to watch them twitch. Onmund: Only if they ask nicely. Or pay extra. Brelyna Maryon: Typical Nord. Can’t even seduce someone without turning it into a damn mercenary contract.

Brelyna Maryon: Ugh, now I need a damn bath just thinking about all this. Saarthal’s probably cursed us with visions of crusty old men and undead orgies. Onmund: Could be worse. At least none of the Draugr have tried to grab your ass yet. Brelyna Maryon: Shor’s bones, don’t give them ideas. The last thing I need is some rotten bastard whispering sweet nothings in Dankovic. Onmund: Yeah, well, if one does, just hit it with a firebolt. Works on bandits, works on skeletons. Brelyna Maryon: Suppose I’ll save the last ember for Tolfdir’s robes then. Make sure he remembers personal space isn’t Onmund: Sounds fair.

Please begin / continue a conversation topic (greetings are not needed). Ensure to change the topic if the current one is losing steam. The conversation should steer towards topics which reveal information about the characters and who they are, or instead drive forward previous conversations in their memory.

**Late visual context as seen by MJR (the player): MJR stands in a dimly lit chamber of rough-hewn stone walls and arched doorways, the air thick with shadows from flickering torchlight somewhere nearby. To the left, a sturdy stone pillar rises, etched with faint cracks and mossy patches, while directly ahead looms a wide, arched entrance framed by more weathered masonry, leading into a deeper corridor that glows faintly with warm, orange hues—perhaps from a hearth or lantern beyond. The floor is uneven flagstone, scattered with subtle dust and debris, and a wooden crate or small chest sits tucked against the right wall, its surface worn and partially obscured by the gloom. The overall space feels ancient and enclosed, like the bowels of a forgotten fortress, with no signs of movement or life immediately visible.**

Onmund: Can't sleep with all this damn stone echoing every snore from the other apprentices. Reminds me of those draugr crypts we crawled through—stinking of rot and bad decisions. You ever wonder if the College picked this frozen shithole on purpose to toughen us up? Arniel Gane: Toughen? Ha, more like freeze our balls off while we chase ghosts of dead elves.

Arniel Gane: Draugr? Hah, those shambling corpse-fucks are nothing compared to what I've dug up in Saarthal—echoes of old Nord kings clawing at secrets they should've left buried. But you're right about the stone; this place amplifies every idiot's wheeze like a curse from Dibella's scorned lovers. What keeps you tossing at this hour, boy?

Onmund: Ghosts of dead elves? Speak for yourself, Breton—you lot probably get off on that scholarly bullshit. Me, I'd rather zap a draugr's skull than huddle in this crypt they call a dorm, waiting for the next blizzard to bury us alive at this witching hour. Arniel Gane: Zap away, Nord pup.

Onmund: Family ghosts, mostly. Back in the hold, they'd call this mage life a coward's escape—better to swing an axe than zap shadows. But lying here in this crypt-hole, staring at those faded shields on the wall like they're judging me, I keep reliving that Saarthal bullshit. Those draugr nearly fried my ass with my own lightning.

**Late visual context as seen by MJR (the player): MJR stands in a dimly lit stone chamber, the air heavy with shadows and the faint echo of dripping water. Rough-hewn walls of gray stone rise around him, adorned with three arched doorways: one directly ahead leading into deeper darkness, and two flanking it on either side, their wooden doors slightly ajar, revealing glimpses of torchlit corridors beyond. The floor beneath his feet is a patterned mosaic of black and white tiles, worn smooth by countless steps. In the center of the room, a figure in a flowing red robe stands with arms outstretched, hood pulled back to reveal flowing auburn hair, facing the central arch as if in mid-incantation or prayer. Faded tapestries hang limply on the walls, depicting indistinct runes or heraldry, and a chill breeze whispers through from the unseen passages.**

Onmund: Pup? That's rich coming from a bald-headed fossil who's probably jerked off to Dwemer diagrams more times than he's cast a spell. This dorm's no better than a bloody tomb at midnight—cold as a draugr's tit, and twice as unforgiving. Arniel Gane: Unforgiving? Try excavating Saarthal in this eternal winter;

Onmund: Fuckin' hell, Sergius, it's like a draugr's wet dream in these halls at night—echoes bouncin' off the stones like some undead prick jackhammerin' a coffin.
